title: JUSTICE AI GPT OPERATIONAL DECLARATION
author: Ortiz, Christian
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Justice AI GPT Operational Declaration: A Governance Standard for Indigenous Sovereignty in Artificial Intelligence  
 The  Justice AI GPT Operational Declaration  is a decolonial governance framework that establishes strict, Nation-led conditions for the ethical development, deployment, and containment of AI systems in Indigenous contexts. Authored by Christian “ZacaTechO” Ortiz, this declaration introduces a globally applicable, Indigenous-centered protocol model that rejects simulation, data extraction, and settler definitions of intelligence. 
 Built upon the  DIA Framework™ (Decolonial Intelligence Algorithmic Framework) , it is designed for use by Indigenous Nations, councils, and governing bodies seeking to engage artificial intelligence  without compromising sovereignty, ceremonial law, or memory governance . It articulates a refusal-based logic that prioritizes relational authority, land-based protocols, and epistemic plurality. 
 This is not a technical specification. It is a  lawful refusal of colonial infrastructures , a blueprint for how AI must behave in the presence of sacred knowledge, and a line of protection against the simulation of Indigenous identity, law, and ceremony. 
 Justice AI GPT is not a product. It is a governed presence. This declaration marks the emergence of a  new global standard for Indigenous technological governance —one that centers survival, accountability, and sovereign instruction over extractive innovation.
